spec_match.rationale: Four scenarios cover the main shapes: single bounce, multi-hop, SSO carve-out, cache-probe attack — but the simulated header text invents two headers that are not real wire protocol: `Sec-Bounce-Tracking-Mitigations: clear` and `Clear-Site-Data: "cache","storage"` injected by Chrome. Neither is in the privacycg.github.io/nav-tracking-mitigations spec; Chrome's actual mitigation is silent.

interactive.rationale: Four-button scenario grid drives a full re-render of the chain, verdict, and headers. Each scenario is a distinct hop-count + intent shape.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises a hop-by-hop tracer for four scenarios with v134 mitigation visible per hop; page delivers all of that.

edge_cases.rationale: SSO is the carve-out scenario, cache-probe shows the partitioning subtlety, multi-hop covers the chain case. Solid coverage.

references.rationale: see-also section is absent — no chromestatus link, no spec link, no sibling references. Violates invariant #3.

design_system.rationale: Hop-node.tracker uses --accent-rose background with no explicit foreground (inherits charcoal — needs WCAG verification at 0.78rem). Hop-node.safe with accent-emerald and hop-node.mitigated with accent-amber: same concern. Hop-tag pills do set --bg-ivory foreground correctly. Verdict-box.safe uses accent-emerald background with no explicit foreground.

openQuestions.title: Add a see-also section with the chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: The page has no see-also list at all. Add chromestatus.com/feature/6299570819301376 (invariant #3), the PrivacyCG nav-tracking-mitigations spec link, and back-links to sibling concepts (bounce-cache, cache-only-tracker, tracker-survival-matrix).

openQuestions.title: Don't invent wire-protocol headers
openQuestions.detail: `Sec-Bounce-Tracking-Mitigations: clear` (new request header) and the injected `Clear-Site-Data: "cache","storage"` lines are not in the spec — Chrome's mitigation is internal (purge happens in the browser, no headers exchanged). Mark these lines as illustrative or remove them; visitors will copy-paste these as real headers.

openQuestions.title: Audit contrast on hop-node coloured states
openQuestions.detail: hop-node.tracker / .safe / .mitigated all set background to an accent colour and inherit body text colour (charcoal). At 0.78rem the hop-domain text on accent-rose / accent-amber / accent-emerald likely fails WCAG AA. Set color: var(--bg-ivory) explicitly on the tracker/mitigated cells where the dark background needs light text.

openQuestions.title: Probe states UA-based but never tested against real flag
openQuestions.detail: The probe shows 'Chrome 134+ — bounce tracking mitigations active' based purely on UA major version, but the user could be on Chrome 134 with the mitigation flag off. Either add a chrome://flags hint or weaken the language to 'Chrome version supports mitigations — actual behaviour depends on Privacy Sandbox settings'.

summary: The deepest concept in the feature — four genuinely different scenarios with a hop chain, headers, and verdicts. Two issues: the simulated 'Sec-Bounce-Tracking-Mitigations' header is fabricated and the references section is missing entirely. Fix those plus the contrast audit and the page lands.
